user_outcome: A SwornAgent operator can trust that stale processes cannot overwrite newer Baton runtime state and that every accepted transition names its authoritative source and revision.
rationale: Loop, MCP, and TUI processes can currently read and rewrite an entire status file without cross-process conflict detection, and state.Read's fail-open behavior is tracked by #52. This slice owns state-snapshot-v1: validated authoritative reads plus content-hash compare-and-write. Runtime transition ownership stays in the loop; S06 removes adapter writes in favor of commands.
in_scope: Define state-snapshot-v1 as a validated Baton status record, authoritative source_ref, and lowercase SHA-256 revision over canonical record bytes.

Implement Repository.Read and CompareAndWrite with required expected revision, transition validation, secure same-directory temporary file, file sync, atomic rename, directory sync where supported, and typed ErrConflict.
Fail closed on malformed JSON, schema-invalid fields, unknown runtime state, unreadable status, symlink substitution, and an authoritative source ref that cannot be resolved.
Migrate loop, scheduler, router, and terminal-outcome writes to the repository so runtime transitions no longer call the unversioned whole-file writer.
Return the new status revision to terminal-outcome-v1 and operation-event-v1 after each accepted transition.
Add a static architecture rule preventing new runtime state writes outside the state repository and approved protocol-planning paths.
out_of_scope: MCP and TUI adapter migration is S06-control-adapter-parity.
Changing Baton slice-status-v1 to carry a Sworn-only numeric revision is prohibited by the release decision.
Git commit ordering for verified verdicts remains covered by C-01 and #93 rather than being inferred from a content revision.
Resume-worktree reset behavior remains in its planned 2026-07-11-contract-edge-gates slice.
Collaborative multi-host storage and remote lock services are outside this repository-local release.
The remaining eleven planned contract-edge-gates slices are excluded.

effort_complexity.rationale: The repository becomes the write authority for a widely used protocol file, requiring strict validation, atomic filesystem semantics, collision tests, and broad caller migration.

risks.description: Different encoders calculate different revisions for logically identical status records.
risks.mitigation: Define revision over the exact validated bytes read from the authoritative source and return the exact post-write bytes/revision from one encoder.

risks.description: Atomic rename preserves content but changes unsafe permissions or follows a symlink.
risks.mitigation: Use secure same-directory creation, reject symlink targets, preserve approved mode, sync before rename, and test substitution/permission cases.

risks.description: Broad caller migration accidentally changes legal Baton transition semantics.
risks.mitigation: Keep state.Transition as the transition oracle, wrap it in CompareAndWrite, and run the existing state and loop transition matrix unchanged.

risks.description: A reader treats an unreadable or invalid record as an empty/planned state.
risks.mitigation: Repository.Read returns typed validation/I/O errors and router/status-lint integration tests prove no role or PASS is produced.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN Repository.Read loads a valid authoritative status record THE SYSTEM SHALL return the validated record with source_ref and its lowercase SHA-256 content revision (C-04, N-01, N-02).
WHEN CompareAndWrite receives the current revision and a legal next state THE SYSTEM SHALL atomically replace the status record and return its new revision (C-04, N-01, N-02).
IF CompareAndWrite receives a stale revision THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return ErrConflict without changing the status bytes (C-04, N-02).
IF Repository.Read encounters malformed, schema-invalid, unknown-state, unreadable, or symlink-substituted status THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return a typed non-PASS error (C-04, N-01; #52).
WHEN two repository instances race from the same expected revision THE SYSTEM SHALL accept exactly one state transition (C-04, N-02).
WHEN the real loop accepts a runtime transition THE SYSTEM SHALL publish the returned status revision in its terminal outcome or operation event (C-01, C-03, C-04, N-01).
THE SYSTEM SHALL contain no runtime status write outside the state repository and explicitly approved planning/protocol paths (C-04, N-02).
